epigraph.text: He has made everything beautiful in its time. He has also set eternity in the human heart.

content.text:
Among the infinite variety of right triangles, two special types 
appear so frequently in geometry, architecture, and nature that their 
properties deserve special attention. The 45-45-90 triangle and the 
30-60-90 triangle have exact, memorable side ratios that allow us to 
solve problems without a calculator. These "special right triangles" 
are the workhorses of geometry.

The 45-45-90 triangle is an isosceles right triangle—a right triangle 
with two equal legs. Since the two acute angles are equal and must sum 
to $90°$, each must measure $45°$.

content.title: 45-45-90 Triangle
content.content:
A **45-45-90 triangle** is a right triangle 
with acute angles of $45°$ each. It is also called an **isosceles 
right triangle**.

content.title: 45-45-90 Triangle Theorem
content.content:
In a 45-45-90 triangle with legs of length $s$:

- Each leg has length $s$

- The hypotenuse has length $s\sqrt{2}$
The ratio of sides is $1 : 1 : \sqrt{2}$.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Since the triangle is isosceles, both legs have the same length $s$. 
By the Pythagorean theorem:
$$
c^2 &= s^2 + s^2 \\
c^2 &= 2s^2 \\
c &= \sqrt{2s^2} = s\sqrt{2}
$$

content.text:
$ can also be written as 
$$\sqrt{2}$ : $\sqrt{2}$ : 2$ by multiplying each term by $$\sqrt{2}$$.}

The 45-45-90 triangle arises naturally when you draw a diagonal of a 
square, bisecting two opposite corners:

content.title: Finding the hypotenuse
content.problem: Find the hypotenuse of a 45-45-90 triangle with leg length 7.
content.solution:
In a 45-45-90 triangle, the hypotenuse equals the leg times $\sqrt{2}$:
\[
\text{hypotenuse} = 7\sqrt{2} \approx 9.899
\]

content.title: Finding the legs from the hypotenuse
content.problem:
A 45-45-90 triangle has hypotenuse $10\sqrt{2}$. Find the length of 
each leg.
content.solution:
If the hypotenuse is $s\sqrt{2}$, then $s\sqrt{2} = 10\sqrt{2}$, so 
$s = 10$.

Each leg has length 10.

content.title: Diagonal of a square
content.problem: Find the diagonal of a square with side length 9.
content.solution:
The diagonal of a square divides it into two 45-45-90 triangles, with 
the diagonal as the hypotenuse:

\[
\text{diagonal} = 9\sqrt{2} \approx 12.73
\]

content.title: Finding the side of a square from its diagonal
content.problem: A square has diagonal 14. Find the side length.
content.solution:
Let $s$ be the side length. Then the diagonal is $s\sqrt{2}$:
$$
s\sqrt{2} &= 14 \\
s &= \frac{14}{\sqrt{2}} = \frac{14}{\sqrt{2}} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{2}} 
 = \frac{14\sqrt{2}}{2} = 7\sqrt{2}
$$

The side length is $7\sqrt{2} \approx 9.899$.

content.text:
The 30-60-90 triangle is half of an equilateral triangle. When you draw 
an altitude in an equilateral triangle, it bisects both the base and the 
vertex angle, creating two congruent 30-60-90 triangles.

content.title: 30-60-90 Triangle
content.content:
A **30-60-90 triangle** is a right triangle 
with acute angles of $30°$ and $60°$.

content.title: 30-60-90 Triangle Theorem
content.content:
In a 30-60-90 triangle where $s$ is the length of the shorter leg 
(opposite the $30°$ angle):

- The shorter leg (opposite $30°$) has length $s$

- The longer leg (opposite $60°$) has length $s\sqrt{3}$

- The hypotenuse (opposite $90°$) has length $2s$
The ratio of sides is $1 : \sqrt{3} : 2$.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Consider an equilateral triangle with side length $2s$. All angles 
measure $60°$. Drawing an altitude from one vertex to the opposite 
side creates two congruent 30-60-90 triangles.

The altitude bisects the base, so the shorter leg equals $s$. The 
altitude bisects the vertex angle, so the angle at the top is 
$60° \div 2 = 30°$, making the other acute angle $60°$.

The hypotenuse is a full side of the equilateral triangle, so it 
equals $2s$.

For the longer leg, use the Pythagorean theorem:
$$
s^2 + (\text{longer leg})^2 &= (2s)^2 \\
s^2 + (\text{longer leg})^2 &= 4s^2 \\
(\text{longer leg})^2 &= 3s^2 \\
\text{longer leg} &= s\sqrt{3}
$$

content.title: Finding all sides given the shorter leg
content.problem: Find all sides of a 30-60-90 triangle with shorter leg 5.
content.solution:
The shorter leg is opposite the $30°$ angle, so $s = 5$.

- Shorter leg: $s = 5$

- Longer leg: $s\sqrt{3} = 5\sqrt{3} \approx 8.66$

- Hypotenuse: $2s = 10$

content.title: Finding sides given the hypotenuse
content.problem: A 30-60-90 triangle has hypotenuse 12. Find the legs.
content.solution:
The hypotenuse is $2s$, so:
\[
2s = 12 \implies s = 6
\]

- Shorter leg: $s = 6$

- Longer leg: $s\sqrt{3} = 6\sqrt{3} \approx 10.39$

content.title: Finding sides given the longer leg
content.problem: A 30-60-90 triangle has longer leg $9\sqrt{3}$. Find the other sides.
content.solution:
The longer leg is $s\sqrt{3}$, so:
\[
s\sqrt{3} = 9\sqrt{3} \implies s = 9
\]

- Shorter leg: $s = 9$

- Longer leg: $9\sqrt{3}$ (given)

- Hypotenuse: $2s = 18$

content.title: Altitude of an equilateral triangle
content.problem: Find the altitude of an equilateral triangle with side length 10.

content.text:
When working with special right triangles, you'll often need to 
simplify radicals and rationalize denominators.

content.title: Rationalizing the denominator
content.problem: A 45-45-90 triangle has hypotenuse 8. Find each leg.

content.title: Another rationalization
content.problem: A 30-60-90 triangle has longer leg 15. Find the shorter leg and hypotenuse.

content.title: Height of a roof
content.problem:
A roof rises at a $30°$ angle and spans 24 feet horizontally from the 
outer wall to the peak. How high is the peak above the wall?

content.title: Area of an equilateral triangle
content.problem: Find the area of an equilateral triangle with side 12.
content.solution:
The altitude of an equilateral triangle with side $s$ is 
$h = \frac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$.

For $s = 12$:
\[
h = \frac{12\sqrt{3}}{2} = 6\sqrt{3}
\]

The area is:
\[
A = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height} 
 = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 12 \cdot 6\sqrt{3} = 36\sqrt{3} \approx 62.35
\]

marginNotes.text:
And God saw everything that he had 
made, and behold, it was very good.

The Lord by wisdom founded the earth; 
by understanding he established the heavens.

I will make justice the measuring line 
and righteousness the plumb line.
